title: Production hardening: correctness and safety fixes
body:
Fix 4 critical issues blocking production readiness:
1. Bit column extraction (Q1 correctness blocker)
2. Race condition on Database shared state
3. Silent error suppression across export/scan paths
4. Auto-detect null bitmap sizes instead of formula-only approach

Scope: format/, engine/, cmd/sdfutil/ packages.
Non-goals: new features, API changes, streaming/pagination.

tasks.title: Fix bit column extraction from bitmap
tasks.body:
## Goal
- Verify and fix bit column value extraction so Q1 returns 44 rows (currently 19)
- Key filters that depend on bit values: ModelLayers.IsControlLayer, SisoRelation.IsLegal, ParametricElements.IsActive

## Context
- format/record.go:120-129 has bit extraction code that reads from bitmap bytes
- computeNullBmpExtra (catalog.go:394-409) computes bitmap size as ceil(colCount/8) + ceil(numBitCols/8) - 1
- ConvertValue (engine/convert.go:71-75) converts []byte{0/1} to bool
- toBool (engine/control_layer.go:62-78) handles bool type
- REMAINING_ISSUES.md suggests bitmap header byte may encode column-count metadata, not null flags

## Investigation Steps
1. Write a diagnostic test that loads ModelLayers table and prints raw IsControlLayer values for each row
2. If all IsControlLayer values are false/nil, the bitmap layout assumption is wrong
3. Examine raw bitmap bytes for ModelLayers records (3 rows expected, 1 with IsControlLayer=true)
4. Compare bitmap bytes against expected values to determine actual layout
5. Fix parseOneRecord bitmap interpretation accordingly

## Acceptance Criteria
- ModelLayers.IsControlLayer correctly returns true for exactly 1 of 3 model layers
- SisoRelation.IsLegal correctly filters legal relations
- Q1 row count increases significantly (target: 44, verify via ExtractControlLayer test)
- All existing tests still pass

## Validation Gates
- go test ./format/... -v -run TestBit
- go test ./engine/... -v -run TestControlLayer
- go test ./... (all pass)

## Key Files
- format/record.go (parseOneRecord, lines 98-153)
- format/catalog.go (computeNullBmpExtra, lines 394-409)
- engine/control_layer.go (queryQ1, toBool)
- engine/convert.go (ConvertValue TypeBit case)

tasks.title: Fix concurrency safety on Database methods
tasks.body:
## Goal
- Ensure all Database public methods that read shared state use proper locking
- Upgrade sync.Mutex to sync.RWMutex for read-heavy access pattern

## Context
- engine/database.go has a sync.Mutex protecting closed and objMapping
- Table() correctly holds lock for objMapping access
- But Tables(), Header(), Catalog(), PageReader(), TotalPages(), PagesForObjectIDs() read fields without lock
- These fields are currently immutable after construction, but the pattern is fragile

## Changes
1. Replace sync.Mutex with sync.RWMutex in Database struct (database.go:14)
2. Use RLock in read-only methods: Tables(), Table(), Header(), Catalog(), PageReader(), TotalPages(), PagesForObjectIDs()
3. Keep exclusive Lock in Close(), BuildObjectMapping(), SetObjectMapping()
4. Ensure no lock is held when calling into format package (avoid deadlock)

## Acceptance Criteria
- All public Database methods use appropriate locking (RLock for reads, Lock for writes)
- No deadlocks (verify: no lock held across package boundaries)
- All existing tests pass
- go vet passes

## Validation Gates
- go vet ./engine/...
- go test ./engine/... -race -count=1
- go test ./... (all pass)

## Key Files
- engine/database.go (Database struct and all methods)

tasks.title: Add error propagation in export and scan paths
tasks.body:
## Goal
- Stop silently dropping errors in sqlite export, CLI export, and record scanning
- Collect and surface errors so callers know what failed

## Changes

### engine/sqlite.go (ExportToSQLite)
- Lines 22-27: Table/Scan errors silently `continue` — collect into error slice, return as warning alongside result
- Lines 36-37: CREATE TABLE error silently skipped — add to error collection
- Lines 49-54: Begin/Prepare errors silently skipped — add to error collection  
- Return (*sql.DB, error) still succeeds with partial data, but add a new ExportResult type or log the errors
- Approach: Add an `errors []error` slice, append to it, and after the loop log/return the count of failures. Don't change the function signature to avoid breaking callers — instead add fmt.Fprintf to stderr or add an ExportToSQLiteWithErrors variant.

### cmd/sdfutil/main.go (cmdExportSQLite)
- Line 377: `insertStmt.Exec(args...)` — check error, rollback tx on failure
- Line 382: `tx.Commit()` — check error, log if failed

### engine/scanner.go (Scan)
- Line 59-61: Malformed row `continue` — this is acceptable for resilience, but log to stderr or count

### format/record.go
- Line 76-78: ParsePageRecords drops parse errors — acceptable (malformed records), but count them
- Line 267-268: ScanTableRecordsMulti drops parse errors — same

## Design Decision
For batch operations (export, scan), we DON'T want to fail on first error. Instead:
- Collect errors and report summary (X tables exported, Y failed)
- For critical path (single table scan), propagate errors immediately
- Use fmt.Fprintf(os.Stderr, ...) for CLI error reporting (already the pattern in main.go)

## Acceptance Criteria
- insertStmt.Exec errors are checked and cause tx.Rollback in cmdExportSQLite
- ExportToSQLite reports per-table failures to stderr
- No silent `continue` on error without at least counting the failure
- All existing tests pass

## Validation Gates
- go vet ./...
- go test ./... (all pass)

## Key Files
- engine/sqlite.go
- cmd/sdfutil/main.go
- engine/scanner.go
- format/record.go

tasks.title: Auto-detect null bitmap sizes with runtime validation
tasks.body:
## Goal
- Verify computeNullBmpExtra produces correct bitmap sizes for all tables
- Add runtime validation that falls back to probing if formula fails
- Remove any remaining hardcoded bitmap size references

## Context
- computeNullBmpExtra (format/catalog.go:394-409) computes from column defs: ceil(colCount/8) + ceil(numBitCols/8) - 1
- The old hardcoded referenceNullBmpExtra map has been removed
- NullBmpExtra is stored in TableDef and passed through to record parsing
- If the formula is wrong for a table, all records for that table will be garbled

## Changes
1. Add a validation function in engine/scanner.go that checks if the first record of a table has valid GUID values (non-zero Data1 for uniqueidentifier columns)
2. If validation fails, try bitmap sizes 0, 1, 2, 3 and pick the one that produces valid GUIDs
3. Cache the validated size back into TableDef.NullBmpExtra
4. Add a test that scans ALL tables in Depropanizer.sdf and verifies bitmap sizes produce valid records

## Acceptance Criteria
- computeNullBmpExtra is verified correct for all 98 tables in Depropanizer.sdf
- If a different .sdf file has tables where the formula fails, the runtime probe finds the correct size
- No regression in existing table parsing
- All existing tests pass

## Validation Gates
- go test ./engine/... -v -run TestBitmapAutoDetect
- go test ./... (all pass)

## Key Files
- format/catalog.go (computeNullBmpExtra)
- engine/scanner.go (add validation/probe logic)
- engine/database.go (TableDef access)
after: Fix bit column extraction from bitmap
